summary: Closed, unmerged WIP cleanup PR centered on Pikachu symbol/header cleanup, with broad fighter source/header organization and several small decompilation moves. The diff renamed many Pikachu prototypes and action-state table references from generic func_8012xxxx names to ftPikachu_* names, added many fighter-specific headers/C stubs or onload/ondeath implementations, adjusted obj_files.mk for per-character source directories and new compiled C files, and renamed repeated HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr references to HSD_JObjUnkMtxPtr. The author later closed it because the PR contained a long repeated list of commits already merged and planned a cleaner replacement PR; there was no substantive reviewer feedback in this slice.
what_changed: Expanded src/melee/ft/chara/ftPikachu/ftpikachu.h from sparse generic declarations to a large set of ftPikachu-prefixed prototypes for pika2 through pika5, including names such as ftPikachu_ActionChange_80124830, ftPikachu_EfSpawn_80124C90, ftPikachu_ZeroVelocity_80124F24, ftPikachu_UpdateVel_80125D80, ftPikachu_CheckProperty_801275CC, ftPikachu_SetState_8012764C, and ftPikachu_PrepCallActionChange_801281EC.

Renamed many Pikachu action-state table function pointers in asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpikachu.s from func_8012xxxx labels to ftPikachu_* symbols, preserving address suffixes while adding role hints like ActionChange, Stub, and PrepCallActionChange.

Performed local C cleanup in Pikachu source files, for example simplifying ftPikachu_UnkHeldItemAction_801244B8 by initializing Fighter *fighter at declaration, switching directly on func_8026B320(fighter->x1974_heldItem), and using if (arg1) instead of if (arg1 != 0).

Moved or enabled additional fighter code in C while trimming matching assembly, notably adding ftIceClimber, ftNana, ftKirby, ftYoshi, ftPurin, ftLuigi, ftSeak, ftZelda, ftDonkey, and ftGigaKoopa C/header pieces and deleting corresponding assembly blocks where applicable.

Updated obj_files.mk to point many fighter source files at per-character directories such as ftMario/ftmario.c, ftFox/ftfox.c, ftLink/ftlink.c, ftPikachu/*.c, ftPichu/ftpichu.c, ftGigaKoopa/ftgigakoopa.c, and to add newly compiled C objects for ftKirby, ftSeak, ftIceClimber, ftYoshi, ftZelda, ftPurin, and ftLuigi.

Renamed HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr to HSD_JObjUnkMtxPtr in include/functions.h and across assembly callsites in eflib, lb collision, mplib, text_2, and sysdolphin/baselib/robj; the evidence shows this was a naming correction rather than a behavior change.

Promoted some assembly labels used as function pointers from lbl_* to func_* globals, such as lbl_8011F060 becoming func_8011F060 in Ice Climbers and lbl_8012BA8C becoming func_8012BA8C in another fighter assembly section, with corresponding @ha/@l relocation references updated.
Added TODO-marked temporary externs for float/data symbols in newly decompiled fighter files, e.g. Ice Climbers notes to remove float externs once asm data is removed.
smart_moves: Kept address suffixes in renamed symbols, making the cleanup searchable and mechanically traceable back to existing map/asm labels while still adding subsystem prefixes and semantic hints.

Updated both declarations and all table/callsite references together; this avoids the common decomp cleanup failure mode where an action-state table still points at the old func_* symbol after a header rename.

Used conservative semantic names only where the diff had obvious behavior cues, such as ActionChange for functions that enter action states, Stub for empty callbacks, EfSpawn for effect-spawning callbacks, ZeroVelocity/UpdateVel for velocity helpers, and SetState/CheckProperty for state/property helpers.
Split decompiled C from residual assembly via obj_files.mk rather than forcing complete file conversion, allowing partial progress while preserving build order and matching references.
When data/float placement was not fully resolved, left explicit TODO externs instead of hiding uncertainty inside fake structs or invented constants.
decomp_lessons: For fighter cleanup PRs, rename symbols in three places as a unit: headers, C definitions, and data/action-state tables in assembly or generated data. Pikachu shows that action callback tables are just as important as prototypes.
Address-suffixed names such as ftPikachu_ActionChange_801250FC are useful transitional names: they encode a likely role without losing exact address identity.
Do not overclaim structure knowledge during partial decompilation. The added Kirby code still uses TODO comments and temporary structs/externs where real data layout was not known.

When converting OnLoad/OnDeath code from asm to C, preserve item registration IDs and fighter flag/field writes exactly; several added fighter C files appear to target small lifecycle functions first because they are bounded and reusable.
Build-system path cleanup can be coupled with decomp work, but large obj_files.mk directory rewrites make PRs noisy; this PR was closed partly because the history/noise was messy.
Global labels used as callbacks should become function symbols, not anonymous lbl_* names, once their addresses are stored in fighter callback fields like x21dc/x21e4.
naming_conventions: Use fighter prefix plus role plus address: ftPikachu_ActionChange_80124BB4, ftPikachu_EfSpawn_80124C90, ftPikachu_PrepCallActionChange_801281EC.
Use ftPikachu_Stub_ADDR for empty/no-op callbacks rather than leaving them as func_ADDR.
Use descriptive helper fragments when behavior is evident: ZeroVelocity, UpdateVel, ActionChangeZeroVel, ActionChangeUpdateVel, CheckProperty, SetState.
Keep HSD/baselib names honest when the exact API meaning is uncertain: HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr was renamed to HSD_JObjUnkMtxPtr rather than pretending the function was fully understood.
Per-fighter source directories use cased folder names such as ftPikachu, ftPichu, ftIceClimber, ftGigaKoopa, ftKirby, ftYoshi, ftPurin, ftLuigi, ftZelda, ftSeak.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promote callback labels from lbl_* to .global func_* and update every relocation pair, e.g. lis/addi references from lbl_8011F060@ha/@l to func_8011F060@ha/@l.
When replacing asm blocks with C, leave the remaining assembly file in obj_files.mk after the new C object so unresolved functions/data can continue to match incrementally.
Use direct switch expressions and minor C expression cleanup only when they preserve the same control flow; the Pikachu held-item helper shows this can simplify code without changing behavior.
Rename cross-cutting external symbols in include/functions.h first, then propagate to all assembly callsites to keep assembler symbol resolution consistent.
Action-state table cleanup is a matching aid: replacing .4byte func_80124B74 with .4byte ftPikachu_80124B74 documents the callback identity without changing table bytes.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in this slice.

The only issue-comment feedback was from the author: the PR had a long list of commits that were already merged, and a squash merge would produce a messy repeated commit list, so the author closed this PR and planned to make a new clean one.
The PR body marked the work as WIP: "probably finish tomorrow and will mark as ready when it's done".
follow_up_queries: Find the clean replacement PR after PR #388 that landed the Pikachu cleanup work.
Check whether HSD_JObjUnkMtxPtr later received a final known name or matching C implementation in baselib/jobj work.
Audit later Pikachu files for whether ftPikachu_ActionChange_* transitional names were replaced with move-specific names.
Look for follow-up PRs removing TODO float externs from ftIceClimber/ftNana and moving corresponding data out of asm.
Compare obj_files.mk after the replacement PR to confirm which per-character directory path changes were actually merged.
